meta.title: Premium Accounting
meta.catalog: Guidewire BillingCenter — Premium Billing Best-Practice Catalog

steps.activity: Home Office receives new policy notification from PAS
steps.output: Email alert to Premium Accounting queue; policy accessible in billing system

steps.activity: Validate premium calculation against rating output
steps.output: Billed premium matched to quote/schedule; variance above SGD 10 flagged

steps.activity: Confirm coverage period and instalment schedule
Verify policyholder banking or card details
steps.output: Bank/card validated via micro-deposit or card check

steps.activity: Configure direct debit mandate in billing system
steps.output: GIRO/direct debit mandate activated; reference recorded in PAS

steps.activity: Set up payment schedule in billing module
steps.output: Due dates, amounts and instalment plan loaded; first due date confirmed
Written confirmation by email/post; amount, due date and payment reference

steps.activity: Notify agent or branch of billing setup completion
steps.output: Agent notified; confirmation logged in CRM

steps.activity: First premium collected on due date
steps.output: Payment deducted; receipt generated and emailed
Receipt matched to policy; GL entry posted in SAP

steps.activity: Update billing status in PAS to 'Active – Current'
steps.output: Policy confirmed fully in-force; agent notified

swim_phases.name: Review & premium validation
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–4 · intake and premium check
swim_phases.note: Policy issuance triggers premium validation; variances above SGD 10 are held for underwriting sign-off.

swim_phases.name: Payment method setup
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–8 · mandate, schedule, confirmation
swim_phases.note: The mandate and payment schedule are configured in Sapiens; the policyholder and agent receive written confirmation.

swim_phases.name: First payment processing
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 9–11 · collection, reconciliation, activation
swim_phases.note: On the due date the first premium is collected, reconciled and posted to the GL; failed payments route to Collections.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-002 against the Guidewire BillingCenter billing catalog

items.name: Automated policy intake
items.detail: PAS-triggered notification into the billing queue aligns with BillingCenter policy-to-billing integration.

items.name: Premium validation vs rating
items.detail: Premium is validated manually against the schedule with a fixed SGD 10 flag. BillingCenter auto-reconciles billed-to-rated premium and raises variance exceptions systematically.

items.name: Instalment schedule configuration
items.detail: Annual to monthly instalment options recorded in the billing module align with BillingCenter payment plans.

items.name: Bank / card verification
items.detail: Micro-deposit / card verification before mandate setup aligns with BillingCenter payment-instrument validation.

items.name: Direct debit mandate setup
items.detail: GIRO/direct-debit mandate activation with reference recorded in PAS aligns with BillingCenter mandate management.

items.name: Instalment fees & partial payments
items.detail: No instalment-fee logic or handling of partial/short payments. BillingCenter applies plan fees and an allocation hierarchy for partial receipts.

items.name: First-payment confirmation
items.detail: Written confirmation with amount, due date and reference aligns with BillingCenter billing communications.

items.name: GST / premium tax handling
items.detail: No explicit GST/premium-tax computation, posting or reporting step. BillingCenter itemises and posts premium tax separately for regulatory reporting.

items.name: Automated collection
items.detail: Scheduled auto-collection with receipt issuance aligns with BillingCenter direct-debit processing.

items.name: Unapplied / suspense cash
items.detail: Receipts are matched manually; there is no suspense/unapplied-cash account for unmatched payments. BillingCenter posts unmatched cash to suspense and works it off systematically.

items.name: Reconciliation to GL
items.detail: GL posting to SAP is performed per policy. BillingCenter posts an automated subledger with daily reconciliation and an exceptions dashboard rather than manual matching.

items.name: Delinquency / dunning
items.detail: Failed first payment is handled manually by Collections. BillingCenter runs an automated delinquency/dunning workflow with staged notices and lapse triggers.

items.name: Write-off / small-balance tolerance
items.detail: No documented small-balance write-off tolerance. BillingCenter auto-writes-off balances below a threshold to avoid uneconomic collection.

items.name: Billing status activation
items.detail: Status set to 'Active – Current' with agent notification aligns with BillingCenter account status management.

critical_missing.title: Unapplied / suspense cash management
critical_missing.detail: Unmatched receipts have no suspense account; manual matching risks misallocation and reconciliation breaks. BillingCenter posts unmatched cash to suspense and works it off with an ageing report.

critical_missing.title: Automated delinquency / dunning
critical_missing.detail: Failed payments are worked manually by Collections with no staged dunning. BillingCenter automates delinquency notices, grace periods and lapse triggers.

critical_missing.title: GST / premium-tax handling
critical_missing.detail: No explicit premium-tax computation, posting or reporting, a regulatory gap versus the catalog's tax itemisation.

critical_missing.title: Write-off & small-balance tolerance
critical_missing.detail: No documented tolerance for uneconomic small balances, leading to manual effort and stale receivables.

control_bars.name: Mandate & schedule
control_bars.note: GIRO mandate and plan setup solid

remediations.title: Introduce suspense / unapplied-cash management
remediations.detail: Post unmatched receipts to a suspense account with an ageing report; auto-match on payment reference and work off exceptions daily. Effort: 4 weeks (Sapiens + SAP).

remediations.title: Automate delinquency / dunning workflow
remediations.detail: Configure staged dunning notices, grace periods and lapse triggers for failed and overdue payments, replacing manual collections handling. Effort: 5 weeks.

remediations.title: Add GST / premium-tax handling
remediations.detail: Compute, itemise and post premium tax separately for regulatory reporting at billing setup. Effort: 3 weeks (Finance + Sapiens).

remediations.title: Automate billed-to-rated reconciliation
remediations.detail: Replace manual premium validation with an automated billed-vs-rated reconciliation that raises only variance exceptions. Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Define instalment fees & small-balance write-off
remediations.detail: Add instalment-fee logic, a partial-payment allocation hierarchy, and an auto-write-off tolerance for uneconomic balances. Effort: 3 weeks.

opt_steps.output: Billed premium auto-reconciled to rating; only variances raised as exceptions

opt_steps.activity: Confirm coverage period and instalment schedule

opt_steps.output: Bank/card validated via micro-deposit or card check

opt_steps.output: Plan loaded with instalment-fee logic and partial-payment allocation
Written confirmation; amount, due date, reference

opt_steps.activity: First premium collected on due date

opt_steps.output: Receipts auto-matched; unmatched cash posted to suspense and aged
Daily subledger posted to SAP; exceptions dashboard worked

opt_steps.activity: Delinquency / dunning on failed or overdue
opt_steps.output: Staged dunning notices, grace periods and lapse triggers
Status set Active – Current; uneconomic balances auto-written-off within tolerance

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Guidewire BillingCenter Premium Billing Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v3.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-002. Alignment to the Guidewire BillingCenter billing catalog increased from 66% to 90%. Critical additions: suspense / unapplied-cash management, an automated delinquency/dunning workflow, GST/premium-tax handling, automated billed-to-rated reconciliation, and a small-balance write-off tolerance.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the BillingCenter catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional controls or automation

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines the process for reviewing newly onboarded policies and establishing premium payment arrangements. Version 3.0 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Guidewire BillingCenter Premium Billing Best-Practice Catalog, achieving 90% alignment (up from 66% in v2.0).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to all personal and commercial lines policies newly issued by Meridian, across online, agent, broker and bancassurance channels, and to mid-term changes that alter billing.
Scope extended: GST/premium-tax computation and suspense-cash handling now apply to every billing setup and receipt.
This procedure does NOT cover claims payment (SOP-INS-007), refund handling (SOP-FIN-012), or reinsurance premium settlements.

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Confirms payment preference at point of sale; submits setup request; addresses billing queries.
Validates premium; configures mandates and plans; applies cash; reconciles to GL; manages suspense.
Runs delinquency/dunning; manages failed payments and reinstatement.
Owns GST posting, GL reconciliation, write-off tolerance and refunds.

sections.title: 5. Cash & Tax Controls (New Section — v3.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the BillingCenter catalog. Suspense-cash handling and premium-tax posting are now mandatory controls on every receipt and billing setup.
Premium tax itemised and posted separately
Unmatched receipts posted to suspense and aged

sections.title: 6. Process Steps
blocks: Confirm coverage period and instalment schedule
Set up payment schedule with instalment fees
First premium collected on due date
Daily subledger to SAP; exceptions worked

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Billing is placed on hold and the issuing agent notified within 1 business day.
Agent or Underwriting confirms the correct premium within 3 business days; endorsement issued if required.
Billing setup resumes only after written Underwriting sign-off on the correct amount.
Failed payments now enter an automated dunning workflow rather than ad-hoc manual follow-up.
Automated dunning notice issued on payment failure; policyholder given a grace period.
Collections contacts the policyholder within 2 business days; 5 business days to provide an alternate method.
If unresolved, a lapse trigger issues a cancellation notice per SOP-INS-011.
Agent notified immediately; policyholder contacted for correction within 1 business day.
Corrected details submitted via secure channel; Premium Accounting re-validates and reactivates billing within 24 hours.
New exception. Unmatched receipts are held in suspense and worked off via an ageing report.
Unmatched receipts posted to the suspense account and aged daily.
Items unresolved after 30 days escalated to Finance for investigation or refund.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: GL subledger, premium-tax and receipt posting

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v3.0: suspense-cash, automated dunning, premium-tax posting, automated reconciliation and write-off tolerance.
Suspense control: unmatched receipts never posted directly to policy; held and aged in suspense.
Automated reconciliation: billed-vs-rated and subledger-to-GL reconciled daily with exceptions worked.
Premium tax: GST itemised and posted separately for regulatory reporting.
Delinquency: staged dunning with documented grace periods and lapse triggers.
Write-off tolerance: uneconomic balances auto-written-off within an approved threshold.
Segregation of duties: setup ≠ cash application ≠ write-off approval.
Immutable audit trail: validation, setup, collection and reconciliation events logged.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Added premium variance exception handling; aligned to SAP S/4HANA migration
OPTIMISED: suspense/unapplied cash; automated dunning; GST handling; automated reconciliation; small-balance write-off; catalog alignment 66% → 90%
